transcript.text: Hi, how are you?
This meeting is being recorded.
I'm doing great.
I feel like I've aged a year since you last spoke to me, even though that was maybe a month and a half ago.
Is it that rough at growthx?
Not that rough, but when I first met you, it was like week two for me, and now it's like week seven.
So the beginning is always when you're just every day learning so much, trying to absorb as much as you can, and trying not to think about it too much so your brain doesn't explode, stuff like that.
No, totally.
I'm checking if Megan can make it.
I know she accepted, I believe, but her calendar has been pretty crazy lately.
I do have Alexis here.
Alexis is our digital marketing manager, so she will also be helping a lot in terms of getting growthx.
She was managing our SEO agency for Context, so she will be helping a lot with the growthx onboarding and doing whatever we can.
Perfect.
Yeah, great to meet you, Alexis.
Yeah, nice to meet you as well.
I think I heard...
And last time we chatted, it was like, yeah, we've got like 60 briefs queued up for their team and nothing's happened yet.

So I think one thing, Alexis, you'll get from GrowthX is just being able to scale with quality a heck of a lot faster and being able to get content produced without as much heavy lifting from your side.
Yeah, I'm excited for that.
Yeah, we're reviewing our drafts and then collaborating very closely, especially like in phase one in the strategy sprint.
That's like the biggest thing that we ask so that, you know, on our side, like we develop these really rich artifacts that give the context necessary for our AI workflows.
And obviously there's experts in the loop, so it's not just like we're pressing a button or anything like that.
But when we have artifacts created that go deep into your personas and your company context and your brand voice, then it allows us to get to quality much faster.
So that's why we're a little bit more collaborative in the first part.
Awesome.
Yes, that's great.
Tyler, would you know the status of the contract?
Sorry, I know I've been looped in on a lot of emails, but there's been so much back and forth.
I think we are more or less set, right?
We're aligned on terms from the communication with Andy and legal.
And Andy, I got him the right version.
He's got the clean version.
And on Monday, he told me that he thinks that legal review could be completed by end of day yesterday or end of day today.
I know Andy has a lot of things going at once.
I can check right now.
I think we're there with legal, to my knowledge.
There's no more terms to get to alignment around.
It's really just a matter of getting the thumbs up so that we can start to get this ready for signature.
Exactly.
Got it.
Okay.
Yeah, you're right.
Legal hasn't approved it yet.
Legal hasn't approved it yet, but you're right.
I think we're just pending that.
And then we're able to sign.
Oh, hi, Megan.
Hey, how's it going?
Good.
Perfect.
Hey, Megan, nice to meet you.
Hey.
What are we here to talk about?
This is a very short meeting and I'm sorry I'm late.
No, you're totally fine.
It was just, it had been a long time since we'd connected with the Redis team.
So one, wanted to get on the same page about where we're at with procurement, ideal kickoff date to set you guys up for.
Two, wanted to answer any questions you had about the partnership and just align on like our priority as soon as we start, which I have a good sense of, but wanted to just confirm with the team here.
Cool.
mean, Fung-Lin's the expert, so I'll pass all the questions over to her.
So I think procurement side, sounds like legal, everything's good.
They just need to complete the final review of the terms that we've agreed to.
What about the finance review?
Has that finished?
That's passed.
That looks like finance review is passed.
Approved.
Passed doesn't approve.
That was vague.
Yeah.
It's just illegal.
Once the legal is done, we're ready to sign.
Okay, great.
And then the signer on the agreement, who's that going to be from the redis side?
It will likely be Angelina, our chief accounting officer.
I believe it's a new process, but I believe we usually prefer, I think we usually prefer to be the ones to sign first and then we'll send it over.
That's fine.
Yeah, that totally works on our side.
No worries there.
And then kickoff dates, I have, looking at the calendar quickly here, I'd actually been holding October 27th for you guys, if you want the earliest date possible.
We want the earliest date.
Yeah.
There's nothing earlier, right?
Because once we get the sign-off, we're ready to go whenever.
Makes sense.
Yeah, I mean, we've had like 10 clients per kickoff date and two kickoffs available.
And so my job sometimes is playing Tetris and Jenga or whatever.
Whatever you Whatever want.
If want to call it, I've been like holding a spot for you guys for a while.
And like, there are a lot of clients who were booking in mid-November.
So I think that that will be the earliest, but if I can get you on.
Yeah, let us know if anything we can do.
Originally, we had planned to kick off.
know like Marcel wanted September 15th, which sounded great.
I know there's been some internal process issues on our side.
We are happy to do like external facing references, but like whatever we can do to cut the line, this is high priority for us for sure.
Yeah.
Okay, sounds good.
So we've effectively turned with our SEO agency.
So there is nothing right now as of now.
So I think that's why for us, otherwise, between now and October 27th, it's just like a waiting.
We're just kind of like on a waiting timeline.
Okay, good to know.
I think as soon as we get the signature from your side, then that obviously makes it really easy for me to go to Marcel, go to the team, say, look, all they need is a signature.
What's their, you know, they turned from their SEO agency.
What's the earliest earliest we can start?
So I'll keep you posted.
just need to check with the team.
In terms of the engagement, you got my email about the resources that we would need from you guys?
Yeah, I did want to ask more.
We have something internally what we use called FlockJ.
FlockJ is kind of like HighSpot or like Sysmic.
Alexis actually had this great idea of like, would it help if we just gave you a license?
And then you guys can pull a bunch of sales docs from there rather than like us exporting and then sending it over.
Yeah, if y'all are okay with that, that sounds great.
And Megan, I did get security approval.
Or Andy said we got all of it, all the access.
Yeah, I think it'll just be easier to plug you guys in.
We have onboarding paths, so like we can help navigate, but probably makes everyone's life easier just to like copy and paste versus export, upload.
That always sucks.
Yeah, there's a whole product solutions library in there that I was telling FlockJ would probably be easier for you guys.
You guys were able to just poke around yourselves and look at that, yeah.
Okay, perfect.
Yeah, I think the person that you'll want to give access to who leads most of the strategy sprints would be, I'm just going to send you his email.
And I'll confirm if there's like an alias that we want to use instead.
But if it was one person, it would be Eric from our team who will be on the strategy sprint kickoff call with Marcel.
Perfect.
Because we have, I can give that access as quick as probably Friday or Monday.
So, yeah, just let me know before Friday if it should go to more of a team alias.
Otherwise, for now, I'll just assume it is going to Eric.
Okay, perfect.
Yeah, and I think if you can get the contract back to us by Friday as well, then that will help out a lot on my side on securing the date.
The questions I wanted to ask quickly, when we were talking about the engagement.
Amen.
Amen.
I think a big goal high level was shift the brand association from caching to AI infrastructure slash vector databases.
Is that still like the North Star in terms of what you're trying to accomplish from a brand perspective?
Yeah, not so much vector databases, but AI infrastructure, and then also just kind of dominating real-time data platform.
Real-time data platform.
Okay, cool.
And the biggest competitor that I had in my notes that you're thinking about was Pinecone.
Is that still accurate?
No, no, I don't think I would say Pinecone is our biggest competitor.
I think our biggest would be Elastic Cache and Memory Store.
Elastic Cache, Valky, Memory Store.
Valky, Memory Store.
Okay, cool.
Just want to pass that in, folks, to my team ahead of time.
And then we talked about net new on editorial and refresh with a need for a lot of refresh, especially up front because of all the quick wins there.
Okay.
Alright.
Bye.
And Fung-Lin, would you say that refresh is where you want us to do as much as we can to start?
Obviously both, but refresh is the...
I know, I was like both.
It was equally important.
I was NetNew, I feel like we went all...
I mean, our NetNew right now is like maybe four a quarter at best.
So I feel like NetNew, honestly, equally important.
Equally important.
So we should balance both when we start creating If faster, if it's faster, yeah.
If NetNew takes longer, then yes, in the short term, maybe refresh.
But at some point, we really want it to next, our content production.
Okay, got it.
So from four per quarter to 40 a quarter.
Yeah, in a perfect way, I that's what we would want to get to.
And then tell me about like review and approval for staging and publishing.
Obviously, you would be involved.
I know that I think it's Allison, right?
On the brand side that would be involved.
I was...
as
We'll probably be more involved in terms of like brand guidelines, like our verbal identity.
But if it's staging and publishing on our CMS, that's probably more Cody.
Okay.
Who you might not remember, but who you also met on week two, on your week two.
I think that was the call before I got here, actually.
So I haven't even met Cody, but I know that Kyle and Marcel have.
Yeah.
And do you know if we'll have access to stage and publish?
Will we be able to actually log in and do that?
That was my understanding from Cody.
I think the goal was that you guys would be able to stage and publish on your own.
Okay, perfect.
I will confirm that with Cody.
But Megan, I think the goal is they would just be able to publish on their own, right?
I think.
I love it.
Yeah.
I mean, let's just make sure we have some oversight.
But for the most part, like whatever we can do to make this as easy as possible.
Yeah.
Perfect.
I believe that's the way Cody had communicated to me before.
Yeah.
But I will confirm.
That all sounds great.
Wait, we don't.
Definitely love to prefer to move fast and obviously keep you in the review cycle, but not have barriers to like being able to get things ready for the last mile execution, hit publish.

The last question I had, we've talked about this before, but just to kind of confirm for the team's preparation, what's the level of like technical expertise that you would need to see in the content that we're creating?

Obviously, we can do that because we work with different technical companies, but just trying to better align to your goal for like the voice and the content and, you know, what kind of content we would be going for.

I would say as technical, as technical as possible, because we are still internally going to be populating like explainer type blogs, like what is X, Y, Z, like those we were still internally handled.
So we were preferring growthx to handle the technical ones.
Okay, perfect.
Sounds good.
And if we did some of.
Both for you?
Would that be of interest?
The better.
The more the better.
Okay, cool.
Any questions for me?
I just wanted to do some housekeeping since we hadn't talked in a while.
I have one quick one.
You mentioned that you would want, you guys would want to interview like a bunch of internal expertise.
Are you guys thinking like, do you need to interview with engineers, like product marketers, like who is it that you typically need to interview so we can set those up?

I would say that if there are any technical experts that are involved in content creation, we would want to talk to those folks for sure, because they already have a pulse on the bar that you're trying to meet with the technical quality and they've been involved before.
I would say product marketers, definitely great folks for us to talk to.

It doesn't have to be like set up, you know, five plus expert interviews, but if we could find that right balance of technical and really knows your ICP product or the content, those are the people that we would benefit them.
I from speaking to at the beginning of the strategy sprint.
Cool.
Fung let's brainstorm on that list because I think we could cast a wide net outside of just product marketing for this one.
Yeah.
I was about to ask who you want because I was thinking obviously product marketers.
was thinking the technical marketing team.
Yeah.
actually was wondering if- Talent.
Yeah.
And I was wondering if like this docs team makes sense or do you want like a- I think someone like that or yeah, like someone on DevRel team.
Oh, DevRel.
Kyle Banker could be interesting actually.
He's not really good in content, but like he is the perfect- Sorry, my dog just made a really weird noise.
He's the perfect kind of intersection of like deep technical expertise.
runs the DevRel team and the applied engineering team and he's very close to the sales cycle.
So like he kind of sees everything.
That's great.
I guess one benefit- there a number?
Is there like a number?
you want to interview up to, like ideally 10 people.
20 people, because yeah, we can bring in as many interviewees.
Let me, let me confirm with Eric.
I think he would know like the right number, but I would say focus on making sure that there's time available for the most important people, right?
Like some of the people that you mentioned, they seem like the most important.
So since we've got some time and this might not start until October 27th, you know, maybe best case I can get you to the 20th.
We'll take the 20th.
Earlier if we can.
If Marcel lets me, like, honestly, right now, I've got Marcel's number.
All right, well, you'll have to tell him, like, Tyler said the 27th, but I need faster.
And then, then maybe, you know, you'll be able to.
Megan, you should then just text for October 10th.
Why wouldn't you just go aim higher?
I love it here.
Yeah.
Go for October 10th, settle on the 20th.
Let's plan for the 27th, unless Marcel tells Megan otherwise.
Let's do that.
I'm going to bother him now.
But this is, this is.
Perfect.
No, I think we've got a really good plan in place.
Just get that contract over to us as soon as you can, and we'll be able to make things happen much faster.
Okay, great.
Awesome.
Well, we're excited.
Talk to you soon.
you.
Thank you.
Bye.
Bye.

default_summary.markdown_formatted:
## Meeting Purpose

[Sync on GrowthX and Redis partnership status, align on kickoff details, and confirm engagement priorities.](https://fathom.video/share/L8sQCrPWsgyNZvZmx7yUR-VXqKoJEsGt?tab=summary&timestamp=185.0)

## Key Takeaways

  - [Contract pending final legal review; finance approved. Earliest kickoff possible is Oct 27th (potentially earlier).](https://fathom.video/share/L8sQCrPWsgyNZvZmx7yUR-VXqKoJEsGt?tab=summary&timestamp=120.0)
  - [Redis team to provide GrowthX access to FlockJ (internal content platform) for efficient resource sharing.](https://fathom.video/share/L8sQCrPWsgyNZvZmx7yUR-VXqKoJEsGt?tab=summary&timestamp=364.0)
  - [Equal focus on new content creation and refreshing existing content, aiming to scale from 4 to 40 pieces per quarter.](https://fathom.video/share/L8sQCrPWsgyNZvZmx7yUR-VXqKoJEsGt?tab=summary&timestamp=530.0)
  - [GrowthX to produce highly technical content, with Redis handling simpler explainer-type blogs internally.](https://fathom.video/share/L8sQCrPWsgyNZvZmx7yUR-VXqKoJEsGt?tab=summary&timestamp=671.0)

## Topics

### Contract Status and Kickoff Timeline

  - [Legal review pending; finance review passed](https://fathom.video/share/L8sQCrPWsgyNZvZmx7yUR-VXqKoJEsGt?tab=summary&timestamp=120.0)
  - [Angelina (CAO) likely to sign for Redis](https://fathom.video/share/L8sQCrPWsgyNZvZmx7yUR-VXqKoJEsGt?tab=summary&timestamp=246.0)
  - [Earliest kickoff date: October 27th](https://fathom.video/share/L8sQCrPWsgyNZvZmx7yUR-VXqKoJEsGt?tab=summary&timestamp=265.0)
  - [Redis team pushing for earlier start if possible (originally planned for Sept 15th)](https://fathom.video/share/L8sQCrPWsgyNZvZmx7yUR-VXqKoJEsGt?tab=summary&timestamp=313.0)
  - [Tyler to check with Marcel for potential earlier dates (Oct 20th or sooner)](https://fathom.video/share/L8sQCrPWsgyNZvZmx7yUR-VXqKoJEsGt?tab=summary&timestamp=846.0)

### Resource Access and Content Management

  - [Redis to provide GrowthX access to FlockJ (internal content platform)](https://fathom.video/share/L8sQCrPWsgyNZvZmx7yUR-VXqKoJEsGt?tab=summary&timestamp=364.0)
  - [Eric from GrowthX team to receive access (pending confirmation of team alias)](https://fathom.video/share/L8sQCrPWsgyNZvZmx7yUR-VXqKoJEsGt?tab=summary&timestamp=424.0)
  - [GrowthX likely to have direct staging and publishing access in Redis CMS](https://fathom.video/share/L8sQCrPWsgyNZvZmx7yUR-VXqKoJEsGt?tab=summary&timestamp=587.0)
  - [Cody from Redis team to handle CMS-related matters](https://fathom.video/share/L8sQCrPWsgyNZvZmx7yUR-VXqKoJEsGt?tab=summary&timestamp=587.0)

### Content Strategy and Priorities

  - [Shift brand association: from caching to AI infrastructure and real-time data platform](https://fathom.video/share/L8sQCrPWsgyNZvZmx7yUR-VXqKoJEsGt?tab=summary&timestamp=480.0)
  - [Main competitors: Elastic Cache, Memory Store (not Pinecone as previously thought)](https://fathom.video/share/L8sQCrPWsgyNZvZmx7yUR-VXqKoJEsGt?tab=summary&timestamp=505.0)
  - [Equal focus on new content creation and content refresh](https://fathom.video/share/L8sQCrPWsgyNZvZmx7yUR-VXqKoJEsGt?tab=summary&timestamp=530.0)
  - [Goal: Scale from 4 to 40 content pieces per quarter](https://fathom.video/share/L8sQCrPWsgyNZvZmx7yUR-VXqKoJEsGt?tab=summary&timestamp=530.0)
  - [Emphasis on highly technical content from GrowthX](https://fathom.video/share/L8sQCrPWsgyNZvZmx7yUR-VXqKoJEsGt?tab=summary&timestamp=671.0)

### Internal Expertise Interviews

  - [GrowthX to interview Redis internal experts for content insights](https://fathom.video/share/L8sQCrPWsgyNZvZmx7yUR-VXqKoJEsGt?tab=summary&timestamp=731.0)
  - [Potential interviewees: technical experts, product marketers, technical marketing team, DevRel team, docs team](https://fathom.video/share/L8sQCrPWsgyNZvZmx7yUR-VXqKoJEsGt?tab=summary&timestamp=731.0)
  - [Kyle Banker suggested as a key interviewee (DevRel lead, deep technical expertise)](https://fathom.video/share/L8sQCrPWsgyNZvZmx7yUR-VXqKoJEsGt?tab=summary&timestamp=796.0)
  - [Fung-Lin and Megan to compile a list of interviewees](https://fathom.video/share/L8sQCrPWsgyNZvZmx7yUR-VXqKoJEsGt?tab=summary&timestamp=784.0)

## Next Steps

  - [Redis to send signed contract ASAP (target: by Friday)](https://fathom.video/share/L8sQCrPWsgyNZvZmx7yUR-VXqKoJEsGt?tab=summary&timestamp=900.0)
  - [Fung-Lin to provide FlockJ access to Eric/GrowthX team by Friday/Monday](https://fathom.video/share/L8sQCrPWsgyNZvZmx7yUR-VXqKoJEsGt?tab=summary&timestamp=424.0)
  - [Fung-Lin to confirm CMS access details with Cody](https://fathom.video/share/L8sQCrPWsgyNZvZmx7yUR-VXqKoJEsGt?tab=summary&timestamp=587.0)
  - [Fung-Lin and Megan to create list of internal experts for GrowthX interviews](https://fathom.video/share/L8sQCrPWsgyNZvZmx7yUR-VXqKoJEsGt?tab=summary&timestamp=784.0)
  - [Tyler to confirm with Eric the ideal number of internal expert interviews](https://fathom.video/share/L8sQCrPWsgyNZvZmx7yUR-VXqKoJEsGt?tab=summary&timestamp=834.0)
  - [Megan to contact Marcel about potentially earlier kickoff date](https://fathom.video/share/L8sQCrPWsgyNZvZmx7yUR-VXqKoJEsGt?tab=summary&timestamp=892.0)

action_items.description: Follow up on legal approval of contract; facilitate signing by Angelina (CAO)
Set up FlockJ access for Eric @ GrowthX by Fri/Mon; confirm if team alias needed instead
Confirm w/ Cody re GrowthX's ability to stage/publish content directly in CMS
Collab w/ Megan - list internal experts (tech, prod mktg, DevRel, docs) for GrowthX interviews
Confirm w/ Eric ideal # of internal experts to interview; relay to Redis team
Check w/ Marcel re possibility of earlier kickoff (pre-Oct 27); update Redis team
